options.text: Two congruent sides and the included angle

quiz_questions.explanation: SAS (Side- Angle -Side) Similarity If two pairs of corresponding sides are proportional and the included angles are congruent, the triangles are similar. AA ( Angle - Angle ) Similarity If two angles of one Triangle are congruent to two angles of another Triangle , the triangles are similar.

options.text: All three pairs of sides to be congruent
All three pairs of sides to be proportional

quiz_questions.explanation: SSS (Side-Side-Side) Similarity If all three pairs of corresponding sides are proportional, the triangles are similar. SAS (Side- Angle -Side) Similarity If two pairs of corresponding sides are proportional and the included angles are congruent, the triangles are similar.

options.text: Two congruent sides and a proportional angle
Two congruent angles and one proportional side
Two proportional side pairs and a congruent included angle

quiz_questions.explanation: SAS (Side- Angle -Side) Similarity If two pairs of corresponding sides are proportional and the included angles are congruent, the triangles are similar. AA ( Angle - Angle ) Similarity If two angles of one Triangle are congruent to two angles of another Triangle , the triangles are similar.

quiz_questions.question_text: Why does AA work to prove similarity?

options.text: If two angles match, the third must also match (angles sum to 180°)

quiz_questions.explanation: AA ( Angle - Angle ) Similarity If two angles of one Triangle are congruent to two angles of another Triangle , the triangles are similar. SAS (Side- Angle -Side) Similarity If two pairs of corresponding sides are proportional and the included angles are congruent, the triangles are similar.

quiz_questions.question_text: △ABC has angles 50°, 70°, 60°. △DEF has angles 50°, 70°, 60°. They are:

options.text: Similar only if sides are equal

quiz_questions.explanation: Example Given two triangles with angles 40°, 60°, 80° and 40°, 60°, 80°: the triangles are similar by AA (two pairs of angles match). AA ( Angle - Angle ) Similarity If two angles of one Triangle are congruent to two angles of another Triangle , the triangles are similar.

quiz_questions.question_text: If △ABC ~ △DEF, AB/DE = 2/3, and AB = 8, then DE =
quiz_questions.explanation: The correct answer is '12'. The other options — '8', '5.33', and '16' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is NOT a valid similarity postulate?
quiz_questions.explanation: AA ( Angle - Angle ) Similarity If two angles of one Triangle are congruent to two angles of another Triangle , the triangles are similar. SSS (Side-Side-Side) Similarity If all three pairs of corresponding sides are proportional, the triangles are similar.
